children.label: A
children.text: the target normal cost of the plan for the plan year,

children.label: B
children.text: the shortfall amortization charge (if any) for the plan for the plan year determined under subsection (c), and

children.label: C
children.text: the waiver amortization charge (if any) for the plan for the plan year as determined under subsection (e); or

in any case in which the value of plan assets of the plan (as reduced under subsection (f)(4)(B)) equals or exceeds the funding target of the plan for the plan year, the target normal cost of the plan for the plan year reduced (but not below zero) by such excess.

children.label: i
children.text: the present value of all benefits which are expected to accrue or to be earned under the plan during the plan year, plus

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount of plan-related expenses expected to be paid from plan assets during the plan year, over

children.label: B
children.text: the amount of mandatory employee contributions expected to be made during the plan year.

children.heading: Special rule for increase in compensation
children.label: 2 Special rule for increase in compensation
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, if any benefit attributable to services performed in a preceding plan year is increased by reason of any increase in compensation during the current plan year, the increase in such benefit shall be treated as having accrued during the current plan year.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For purposes of this section, the shortfall amortization charge for a plan for any plan year is the aggregate total (not less than zero) of the shortfall amortization installments for such plan year with respect to any shortfall amortization base which has not been fully amortized under this subsection.

children.heading: Determination
children.label: A Determination
children.text: The shortfall amortization installments are the amounts necessary to amortize the shortfall amortization base of the plan for any plan year in level annual installments over the 7-plan-year period beginning with such plan year.

children.heading: Shortfall installment
children.label: B Shortfall installment
children.text: The shortfall amortization installment for any plan year in the 7-plan-year period under subparagraph (A) with respect to any shortfall amortization base is the annual installment determined under subparagraph (A) for that year for that base.

children.heading: Segment rates
children.label: C Segment rates
children.text: In determining any shortfall amortization installment under this paragraph, the plan sponsor shall use the segment rates determined under subparagraph (C) of subsection (h)(2), applied under rules similar to the rules of subparagraph (B) of subsection (h)(2).

children.heading: Special election for eligible plan years
children.label: D Special election for eligible plan years
I
children.text: the shortfall amortization installments with respect to such base shall be determined under clause (ii) or (iii), whichever is specified in the election, and

children.label: II
children.text: the shortfall amortization installment for any plan year in the 9-plan-year period described in clause (ii) or the 15-plan-year period described in clause (iii), respectively, with respect to such shortfall amortization base is the annual installment determined under the applicable clause for that year for that base.

children.label: I
children.text: in the case of the first 2 plan years in the 9-plan-year period beginning with the election year, interest on the shortfall amortization base of the plan for the election year (determined using the effective interest rate for the plan for the election year), and

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of the last 7 plan years in such 9-plan-year period, the amounts necessary to amortize the remaining balance of the shortfall amortization base of the plan for the election year in level annual installments over such last 7 plan years (using the segment rates under subparagraph (C) for the election year).

children.heading: 15-year amortization
children.label: iii 15-year amortization
children.text: The shortfall amortization installments determined under this subparagraph are the amounts necessary to amortize the shortfall amortization base of the plan for the election year in level annual installments over the 15-plan-year period beginning with the election year (using the segment rates under subparagraph (C) for the election year).

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: The plan sponsor of a plan may elect to have this subparagraph apply to not more than 2 eligible plan years with respect to the plan, except that in the case of a plan described in section 106 of the Pension Protection Act of 2006, the plan sponsor may only elect to have this subparagraph apply to a plan year beginning in 2011.

children.heading: Amortization schedule
children.label: II Amortization schedule
children.text: Such election shall specify whether the amortization schedule under clause (ii) or (iii) shall apply to an election year, except that if a plan sponsor elects to have this subparagraph apply to 2 eligible plan years, the plan sponsor must elect the same schedule for both years.

children.heading: Other rules
children.label: III Other rules
children.text: Such election shall be made at such time, and in such form and manner, as shall be prescribed by the Secretary of the Treasury, and may be revoked only with the consent of the Secretary of the Treasury. The Secretary of the Treasury shall, before granting a revocation request, provide the Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation an opportunity to comment on the conditions applicable to the treatment of any portion of the election year shortfall amortization base that remains unamortized as of the revocation date.

children.heading: Eligible plan year
children.label: v Eligible plan year
children.text: For purposes of this subparagraph, the term “eligible plan year” means any plan year beginning in 2008, 2009, 2010, or 2011, except that a plan year shall only be treated as an eligible plan year if the due date under subsection (j)(1) for the payment of the minimum required contribution for such plan year occurs on or after .June 25, 2010

children.label: I
children.text: give notice of the election to participants and beneficiaries of the plan, and

children.label: II
children.text: inform the Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation of such election in such form and manner as the Director of the Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation may prescribe.

children.heading: Increases in required installments in certain cases
children.label: vii Increases in required installments in certain cases
children.text: For increases in required contributions in cases of excess compensation or extraordinary dividends or stock redemptions, see paragraph (7).

children.label: A
children.text: the funding shortfall of such plan for such plan year, minus

children.label: B
children.text: the present value (determined using the segment rates determined under subparagraph (C) of subsection (h)(2), applied under rules similar to the rules of subparagraph (B) of subsection (h)(2)) of the aggregate total of the shortfall amortization installments and waiver amortization installments which have been determined for such plan year and any succeeding plan year with respect to the shortfall amortization bases and waiver amortization bases of the plan for any plan year preceding such plan year.

children.label: A
children.text: the funding target of the plan for the plan year, over

children.label: B
children.text: the value of plan assets of the plan (as reduced under subsection (f)(4)(B)) for the plan year which are held by the plan on the valuation date.

children.heading: Exemption from new shortfall amortization base
children.label: 5 Exemption from new shortfall amortization base
children.text: In any case in which the value of plan assets of the plan (as reduced under subsection (f)(4)(A)) is equal to or greater than the funding target of the plan for the plan year, the shortfall amortization base of the plan for such plan year shall be zero.

children.heading: Early deemed amortization upon attainment of funding target
children.label: 6 Early deemed amortization upon attainment of funding target
children.text: In any case in which the funding shortfall of a plan for a plan year is zero, for purposes of determining the shortfall amortization charge for such plan year and succeeding plan years, the shortfall amortization bases for all preceding plan years (and all shortfall amortization installments determined with respect to such bases) shall be reduced to zero.

children.heading: Increases in alternate required installments in cases of excess compensation or extraordinary dividends or stock redemptions
children.label: 7 Increases in alternate required installments in cases of excess compensation or extraordinary dividends or stock redemptions

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If there is an installment acceleration amount with respect to a plan for any plan year in the restriction period with respect to an election year under paragraph (2)(D), then the shortfall amortization installment otherwise determined and payable under such paragraph for such plan year shall, subject to the limitation under subparagraph (B), be increased by such amount.

children.heading: Total installments limited to shortfall base
children.label: B Total installments limited to shortfall base
i
children.text: such increase shall not result in the amount of such installment exceeding the present value of such installment and all succeeding installments with respect to such base (determined without regard to such increase but after application of clause (ii)), and

children.label: ii
children.text: subsequent shortfall amortization installments with respect to such base shall, in reverse order of the otherwise required installments, be reduced to the extent necessary to limit the present value of such subsequent shortfall amortization installments (after application of this paragraph) to the present value of the remaining unamortized shortfall amortization base.

children.label: I
children.text: the aggregate amount of excess employee compensation determined under subparagraph (D) with respect to all employees for the plan year, plus

children.label: II
children.text: the aggregate amount of extraordinary dividends and redemptions determined under subparagraph (E) for the plan year.

children.label: I
children.text: the sum of the shortfall amortization installments for the plan year and all preceding plan years in the amortization period elected under paragraph (2)(D) with respect to the shortfall amortization base with respect to an election year, determined without regard to paragraph (2)(D) and this paragraph, over

children.label: II
children.text: the sum of the shortfall amortization installments for such plan year and all such preceding plan years, determined after application of paragraph (2)(D) (and in the case of any preceding plan year, after application of this paragraph).

children.heading: Carryover of excess installment acceleration amounts
children.label: iii Carryover of excess installment acceleration amounts

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: If the installment acceleration amount for any plan year (determined without regard to clause (ii)) exceeds the limitation under clause (ii), then, subject to subclause (II), such excess shall be treated as an installment acceleration amount with respect to the succeeding plan year.

children.heading: Cap to apply
children.label: II Cap to apply
children.text: If any amount treated as an installment acceleration amount under subclause (I) or this subclause with respect any succeeding plan year, when added to other installment acceleration amounts (determined without regard to clause (ii)) with respect to the plan year, exceeds the limitation under clause (ii), the portion of such amount representing such excess shall be treated as an installment acceleration amount with respect to the next succeeding plan year.1So in original. Probably should be followed by “to”.1

children.heading: Limitation on years to which amounts carried for
children.label: III Limitation on years to which amounts carried for
children.text: No amount shall be carried under subclause (I) or (II) to a plan year which begins after the first plan year following the last plan year in the restriction period (or after the second plan year following such last plan year in the case of an election year with respect to which 15-year amortization was elected under paragraph (2)(D)).

children.heading: Ordering rules
children.label: IV Ordering rules
children.text: For purposes of applying subclause (II), installment acceleration amounts for the plan year (determined without regard to any carryover under this clause) shall be applied first against the limitation under clause (ii) and then carryovers to such plan year shall be applied against such limitation on a first-in, first-out basis.

children.label: I
children.text: the aggregate amount includible in income under chapter 1 of title 26 for remuneration during the calendar year in which such plan year begins for services performed by the employee for the plan sponsor (whether or not performed during such calendar year), over

children.heading: Amounts set aside for nonqualified deferred compensation
children.label: ii Amounts set aside for nonqualified deferred compensation
children.text: If during any calendar year assets are set aside or reserved (directly or indirectly) in a trust (or other arrangement as determined by the Secretary of the Treasury), or transferred to such a trust or other arrangement, by a plan sponsor for purposes of paying deferred compensation of an employee under a nonqualified deferred compensation plan (as defined in section 409A of such title) of the plan sponsor, then, for purposes of clause (i), the amount of such assets shall be treated as remuneration of the employee includible in income for the calendar year unless such amount is otherwise includible in income for such year. An amount to which the preceding sentence applies shall not be taken into account under this paragraph for any subsequent calendar year.

children.heading: Only remuneration for certain post-2009 services counted
children.label: iii Only remuneration for certain post-2009 services counted
children.text: Remuneration shall be taken into account under clause (i) only to the extent attributable to services performed by the employee for the plan sponsor after .February 28, 2010

children.heading: Exception for certain equity payments
children.label: iv Exception for certain equity payments

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: There shall not be taken into account under clause (i)(I) any amount includible in income with respect to the granting after , of service recipient stock (within the meaning of ) that, upon such grant, is subject to a substantial risk of forfeiture (as defined under section 83(c)(1) of such title) for at least 5 years from the date of such grant.February 28, 2010section 409A of title 26

children.heading: Secretarial authority
children.label: II Secretarial authority
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury may by regulation provide for the application of this clause in the case of a person other than a corporation.

children.heading: Commissions
children.label: I Commissions
children.text: Any remuneration payable on a commission basis solely on account of income directly generated by the individual performance of the individual to whom such remuneration is payable.

children.heading: Certain payments under existing contracts
children.label: II Certain payments under existing contracts
children.text: Any remuneration consisting of nonqualified deferred compensation, restricted stock, stock options, or stock appreciation rights payable or granted under a written binding contract that was in effect on , and which was not modified in any material respect before such remuneration is paid.March 1, 2010

children.heading: Self-employed individual treated as employee
children.label: vi Self-employed individual treated as employee
children.text: The term “employee” includes, with respect to a calendar year, a self-employed individual who is treated as an employee under section 401(c) of such title for the taxable year ending during such calendar year, and the term “compensation” shall include earned income of such individual with respect to such self-employment.

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of a plan sponsor that determined and declared dividends in the same manner for at least 5 consecutive years immediately preceding such plan year, the aggregate amount of dividends determined and declared for such plan year using such manner.

children.heading: Only certain post-2009 dividends and redemptions counted
children.label: ii Only certain post-2009 dividends and redemptions counted
children.text: For purposes of clause (i), there shall only be taken into account dividends declared, and redemptions occurring, after .February 28, 2010

children.heading: Exception for intra-group dividends
children.label: iii Exception for intra-group dividends
children.text: Dividends paid by one member of a controlled group (as defined in ) to another member of such group shall not be taken into account under clause (i).section 1082(d)(3) of this title

children.heading: Exception for certain redemptions
children.label: iv Exception for certain redemptions
children.text: Redemptions that are made pursuant to a plan maintained with respect to employees, or that are made on account of the death, disability, or termination of employment of an employee or shareholder, shall not be taken into account under clause (i).

children.heading: Exception for certain preferred stock
children.label: v Exception for certain preferred stock

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: Dividends and redemptions with respect to applicable preferred stock shall not be taken into account under clause (i) to the extent that dividends accrue with respect to such stock at a specified rate in all events and without regard to the plan sponsor’s income, and interest accrues on any unpaid dividends with respect to such stock.

children.heading: Applicable preferred stock
children.label: II Applicable preferred stock
children.text: For purposes of subclause (I), the term “applicable preferred stock” means preferred stock which was issued before (or which was issued after such date and is held by an employee benefit plan subject to the provisions of this subchapter).March 1, 2010

children.heading: Plan sponsor
children.label: i Plan sponsor
children.text: The term “plan sponsor” includes any member of the plan sponsor’s controlled group (as defined in ).section 1082(d)(3) of this title

children.heading: Elections for multiple plans
children.label: iii Elections for multiple plans
children.text: If a plan sponsor makes elections under paragraph (2)(D) with respect to 2 or more plans, the Secretary of the Treasury shall provide rules for the application of this paragraph to such plans, including rules for the ratable allocation of any installment acceleration amount among such plans on the basis of each plan’s relative reduction in the plan’s shortfall amortization installment for the first plan year in the amortization period described in subparagraph (A) (determined without regard to this paragraph).

children.heading: Mergers and acquisitions
children.label: iv Mergers and acquisitions
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury shall prescribe rules for the application of paragraph (2)(D) and this paragraph in any case where there is a merger or acquisition involving a plan sponsor making the election under paragraph (2)(D).

children.label: A

children.heading: Rules relating to funding target
children.label: d Rules relating to funding target

children.heading: Funding target
children.label: 1 Funding target
children.text: Except as provided in subsection (i)(1) with respect to plans in at-risk status, the funding target of a plan for a plan year is the present value of all benefits accrued or earned under the plan as of the beginning of the plan year.

children.label: A
children.text: the value of plan assets for the plan year (as reduced under subsection (f)(4)(B)), bears to

children.label: B
children.text: the funding target of the plan for the plan year (determined without regard to subsection (i)(1)).

children.heading: Determination of waiver amortization charge
children.label: 1 Determination of waiver amortization charge
children.text: The waiver amortization charge (if any) for a plan for any plan year is the aggregate total of the waiver amortization installments for such plan year with respect to the waiver amortization bases for each of the 5 preceding plan years.

children.heading: Determination
children.label: A Determination
children.text: The waiver amortization installments are the amounts necessary to amortize the waiver amortization base of the plan for any plan year in level annual installments over a period of 5 plan years beginning with the succeeding plan year.

children.heading: Waiver installment
children.label: B Waiver installment
children.text: The waiver amortization installment for any plan year in the 5-year period under subparagraph (A) with respect to any waiver amortization base is the annual installment determined under subparagraph (A) for that year for that base.

children.heading: Interest rate
children.label: 3 Interest rate
children.text: In determining any waiver amortization installment under this subsection, the plan sponsor shall use the segment rates determined under subparagraph (C) of subsection (h)(2), applied under rules similar to the rules of subparagraph (B) of subsection (h)(2).

children.heading: Waiver amortization base
children.label: 4 Waiver amortization base
children.text: The waiver amortization base of a plan for a plan year is the amount of the waived funding deficiency (if any) for such plan year under .section 1082(c) of this title

children.heading: Early deemed amortization upon attainment of funding target
children.label: 5 Early deemed amortization upon attainment of funding target
children.text: In any case in which the funding shortfall of a plan for a plan year is zero, for purposes of determining the waiver amortization charge for such plan year and succeeding plan years, the waiver amortization bases for all preceding plan years (and all waiver amortization installments determined with respect to such bases) shall be reduced to zero.

children.heading: Reduction of minimum required contribution by prefunding balance and funding standard carryover balance
children.label: f Reduction of minimum required contribution by prefunding balance and funding standard carryover balance

children.heading: Prefunding balance
children.label: A Prefunding balance
children.text: The plan sponsor of a single-employer plan may elect to maintain a prefunding balance.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: In the case of a single-employer plan described in clause (ii), the plan sponsor may elect to maintain a funding standard carryover balance, until such balance is reduced to zero.

children.heading: Election to apply balances against minimum required contribution
children.label: 3 Election to apply balances against minimum required contribution

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraphs (B) and (C), in the case of any plan year in which the plan sponsor elects to credit against the minimum required contribution for the current plan year all or a portion of the prefunding balance or the funding standard carryover balance for the current plan year (not in excess of such minimum required contribution), the minimum required contribution for the plan year shall be reduced as of the first day of the plan year by the amount so credited by the plan sponsor. For purposes of the preceding sentence, the minimum required contribution shall be determined after taking into account any waiver under .section 1082(c) of this title

children.heading: Coordination with funding standard carryover balance
children.label: B Coordination with funding standard carryover balance
children.text: To the extent that any plan has a funding standard carryover balance greater than zero, no amount of the prefunding balance of such plan may be credited under this paragraph in reducing the minimum required contribution.

children.label: iii Limitation to charities
children.text: This subparagraph shall not apply to any plan unless such plan is maintained exclusively by one or more organizations described in .section 501(c)(3) of title 26

children.heading: Effect of balances on amounts treated as value of plan assets
children.label: 4 Effect of balances on amounts treated as value of plan assets

children.heading: Applicability of shortfall amortization base
children.label: A Applicability of shortfall amortization base
children.text: For purposes of subsection (c)(5), the value of plan assets is deemed to be such amount, reduced by the amount of the prefunding balance, but only if an election under paragraph (3) applying any portion of the prefunding balance in reducing the minimum required contribution is in effect for the plan year.

children.heading: Determination of excess assets, funding shortfall, and funding target attainment percentage
children.label: B Determination of excess assets, funding shortfall, and funding target attainment percentage

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: For purposes of subsections (a), (c)(4)(B), and (d)(2)(A), the value of plan assets is deemed to be such amount, reduced by the amount of the prefunding balance and the funding standard carryover balance.

children.heading: Special rule for certain binding agreements with PBGC
children.label: ii Special rule for certain binding agreements with PBGC
children.text: For purposes of subsection (c)(4)(B), the value of plan assets shall not be deemed to be reduced for a plan year by the amount of the specified balance if, with respect to such balance, there is in effect for a plan year a binding written agreement with the Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation which provides that such balance is not available to reduce the minimum required contribution for the plan year. For purposes of the preceding sentence, the term “specified balance” means the prefunding balance or the funding standard carryover balance, as the case may be.

children.heading: Availability of balances in plan year for crediting against minimum required contribution
children.label: C Availability of balances in plan year for crediting against minimum required contribution
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (3)(C)(i) of this subsection, the value of plan assets is deemed to be such amount, reduced by the amount of the prefunding balance.

children.heading: Election to reduce balance prior to determinations of value of plan assets and crediting against minimum required contribution
children.label: 5 Election to reduce balance prior to determinations of value of plan assets and crediting against minimum required contribution

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The plan sponsor may elect to reduce by any amount the balance of the prefunding balance and the funding standard carryover balance for any plan year (but not below zero). Such reduction shall be effective prior to any determination of the value of plan assets for such plan year under this section and application of the balance in reducing the minimum required contribution for such plan for such plan year pursuant to an election under paragraph (2).2So in original. Probably should be “paragraph (3).”2

children.heading: Coordination between prefunding balance and funding standard carryover balance
children.label: B Coordination between prefunding balance and funding standard carryover balance
children.text: To the extent that any plan has a funding standard carryover balance greater than zero, no election may be made under subparagraph (A) with respect to the prefunding balance.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A prefunding balance maintained by a plan shall consist of a beginning balance of zero, increased and decreased to the extent provided in subparagraphs (B) and (C), and adjusted further as provided in paragraph (8).

children.label: I

children.label: II
children.text: the minimum required contribution for such preceding plan year.

children.heading: Adjustments for interest
children.label: ii Adjustments for interest
children.text: Any excess contributions under clause (i) shall be properly adjusted for interest accruing for the periods between the first day of the current plan year and the dates on which the excess contributions were made, determined by using the effective interest rate for the preceding plan year and by treating contributions as being first used to satisfy the minimum required contribution.

children.heading: Certain contributions necessary to avoid benefit limitations disregarded
children.label: iii Certain contributions necessary to avoid benefit limitations disregarded
children.text: The excess described in clause (i) with respect to any preceding plan year shall be reduced (but not below zero) by the amount of contributions an employer would be required to make under paragraph (1), (2), or (4) of to avoid a benefit limitation which would otherwise be imposed under such paragraph for the preceding plan year. Any contribution which may be taken into account in satisfying the requirements of more than 1 of such paragraphs shall be taken into account only once for purposes of this clause.section 1056(g) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A funding standard carryover balance maintained by a plan shall consist of a beginning balance determined under subparagraph (B), decreased to the extent provided in subparagraph (C), and adjusted further as provided in paragraph (8).

children.heading: Beginning balance
children.label: B Beginning balance
children.text: The beginning balance of the funding standard carryover balance shall be the positive balance described in paragraph (1)(B)(ii)(II).

children.label: i

children.label: ii
children.text: as of the time specified in paragraph (5)(A), any reduction in such balance elected under paragraph (5).

children.heading: Adjustments for investment experience
children.label: 8 Adjustments for investment experience
children.text: In determining the prefunding balance or the funding standard carryover balance of a plan as of the first day of the plan year, the plan sponsor shall, in accordance with regulations prescribed by the Secretary of the Treasury, adjust such balance to reflect the rate of return on plan assets for the preceding plan year. Notwithstanding subsection (g)(3), such rate of return shall be determined on the basis of fair market value and shall properly take into account, in accordance with such regulations, all contributions, distributions, and other plan payments made during such period.

children.heading: Elections
children.label: 9 Elections
children.text: Elections under this subsection shall be made at such times, and in such form and manner, as shall be prescribed in regulations of the Secretary of the Treasury.

children.heading: Valuation of plan assets and liabilities
children.label: g Valuation of plan assets and liabilities

children.heading: Timing of determinations
children.label: 1 Timing of determinations
children.text: Except as otherwise provided under this subsection, all determinations under this section for a plan year shall be made as of the valuation date of the plan for such plan year.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), the valuation date of a plan for any plan year shall be the first day of the plan year.

children.heading: Exception for small plans
children.label: B Exception for small plans
children.text: If, on each day during the preceding plan year, a plan had 100 or fewer participants, the plan may designate any day during the plan year as its valuation date for such plan year and succeeding plan years. For purposes of this subparagraph, all defined benefit plans which are single-employer plans and are maintained by the same employer (or any member of such employer’s controlled group) shall be treated as 1 plan, but only participants with respect to such employer or member shall be taken into account.

children.heading: Application of certain rules in determination of plan size
children.label: C Application of certain rules in determination of plan size

children.heading: Plans not in existence in preceding year
children.label: i Plans not in existence in preceding year
children.text: In the case of the first plan year of any plan, subparagraph (B) shall apply to such plan by taking into account the number of participants that the plan is reasonably expected to have on days during such first plan year.

children.heading: Predecessors
children.label: ii Predecessors
children.text: Any reference in subparagraph (B) to an employer shall include a reference to any predecessor of such employer.

children.heading: Determination of value of plan assets
children.label: 3 Determination of value of plan assets

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), the value of plan assets shall be the fair market value of the assets.

children.label: i

children.heading: Effective interest rate
children.label: A Effective interest rate
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “effective interest rate” means, with respect to any plan for any plan year, the single rate of interest which, if used to determine the present value of the plan’s accrued or earned benefits referred to in subsection (d)(1), would result in an amount equal to the funding target of the plan for such plan year.

children.heading: Interest rates for determining funding target
children.label: B Interest rates for determining funding target
i
children.text: in the case of benefits reasonably determined to be payable during the 5-year period beginning on the valuation date for the plan year, the first segment rate with respect to the applicable month,

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of benefits reasonably determined to be payable during the 15-year period beginning at the end of the period described in clause (i), the second segment rate with respect to the applicable month, and

children.label: iii
children.text: in the case of benefits reasonably determined to be payable after the period described in clause (ii), the third segment rate with respect to the applicable month.

children.heading: First segment rate
children.label: i First segment rate
children.text: The term “first segment rate” means, with respect to any month, the single rate of interest which shall be determined by the Secretary of the Treasury for such month on the basis of the corporate bond yield curve for such month, taking into account only that portion of such yield curve which is based on bonds maturing during the 5-year period commencing with such month.

children.heading: Second segment rate
children.label: ii Second segment rate
children.text: The term “second segment rate” means, with respect to any month, the single rate of interest which shall be determined by the Secretary of the Treasury for such month on the basis of the corporate bond yield curve for such month, taking into account only that portion of such yield curve which is based on bonds maturing during the 15-year period beginning at the end of the period described in clause (i).

children.heading: Third segment rate
children.label: iii Third segment rate
children.text: The term “third segment rate” means, with respect to any month, the single rate of interest which shall be determined by the Secretary of the Treasury for such month on the basis of the corporate bond yield curve for such month, taking into account only that portion of such yield curve which is based on bonds maturing during periods beginning after the period described in clause (ii).

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: If a segment rate described in clause (i), (ii), or (iii) with respect to any applicable month (determined without regard to this clause) is less than the applicable minimum percentage, or more than the applicable maximum percentage, of the average of the segment rates described in such clause for years in the 25-year period ending with September 30 of the calendar year preceding the calendar year in which the plan year begins, then the segment rate described in such clause with respect to the applicable month shall be equal to the applicable minimum percentage or the applicable maximum percentage of such average, whichever is closest. The Secretary of the Treasury shall determine such average on an annual basis and may prescribe equivalent rates for years in any such 25-year period for which the rates described in any such clause are not available. Notwithstanding anything in this subclause, if the average of the first, second, or third segment rate for any 25-year period is less than 5 percent, such average shall be deemed to be 5 percent.

children.heading: Applicable minimum percentage; applicable maximum percentage
children.label: II Applicable minimum percentage; applicable maximum percentage
children.text: For purposes of subclause (I), the applicable minimum percentage and the applicable maximum percentage for a plan year beginning in a calendar year shall be determined in accordance with the following table:If the calendar year is:Theapplicable minimum percentage is:Theapplicable maximum percentage is:Any year in the period starting in 2012 and ending in 201990%110%Any year in the period starting in 2020 and ending in 203095%105%203190%110%203285%115%203380%120%203475%125%After 203470%130%.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The term “corporate bond yield curve” means, with respect to any month, a yield curve which is prescribed by the Secretary of the Treasury for such month and which reflects the average, for the 24-month period ending with the month preceding such month, of monthly yields on investment grade corporate bonds with varying maturities and that are in the top 3 quality levels available.

children.heading: Election to use yield curve
children.label: ii Election to use yield curve
children.text: Solely for purposes of determining the minimum required contribution under this section, the plan sponsor may, in lieu of the segment rates determined under subparagraph (C), elect to use interest rates under the corporate bond yield curve. For purposes of the preceding sentence such curve shall be determined without regard to the 24-month averaging described in clause (i). Such election, once made, may be revoked only with the consent of the Secretary of the Treasury.

children.heading: Applicable month
children.label: E Applicable month
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “applicable month” means, with respect to any plan for any plan year, the month which includes the valuation date of such plan for such plan year or, at the election of the plan sponsor, any of the 4 months which precede such month. Any election made under this subparagraph shall apply to the plan year for which the election is made and all succeeding plan years, unless the election is revoked with the consent of the Secretary of the Treasury.

children.heading: Publication requirements
children.label: F Publication requirements
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury shall publish for each month the corporate bond yield curve (and the corporate bond yield curve reflecting the modification described in section 1055(g)(3)(B)(iii)(I) of this title for such month) and each of the rates determined under subparagraph (C) and the averages determined under subparagraph (C)(iv) for such month. The Secretary of the Treasury shall also publish a description of the methodology used to determine such yield curve and such rates which is sufficiently detailed to enable plans to make reasonable projections regarding the yield curve and such rates for future months based on the plan’s projection of future interest rates.3See References in Text note below.3

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (C) or (D), the Secretary of the Treasury shall by regulation prescribe mortality tables to be used in determining any present value or making any computation under this section. Such tables shall be based on the actual experience of pension plans and projected trends in such experience. In prescribing such tables, the Secretary of the Treasury shall take into account results of available independent studies of mortality of individuals covered by pension plans.

children.heading: Periodic revision
children.label: B Periodic revision
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury shall (at least every 10 years) make revisions in any table in effect under subparagraph (A) to reflect the actual experience of pension plans and projected trends in such experience.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Upon request by the plan sponsor and approval by the Secretary of the Treasury, a mortality table which meets the requirements of clause (iii) shall be used in determining any present value or making any computation under this section during the period of consecutive plan years (not to exceed 10) specified in the request.

children.heading: Deadline for submission and disposition of application
children.label: v Deadline for submission and disposition of application

children.heading: Submission
children.label: I Submission
children.text: The plan sponsor shall submit a mortality table to the Secretary of the Treasury for approval under this subparagraph at least 7 months before the 1st day of the period described in clause (i).

children.heading: Disposition
children.label: II Disposition
children.text: Any mortality table submitted to the Secretary of the Treasury for approval under this subparagraph shall be treated as in effect as of the 1st day of the period described in clause (i) unless the Secretary of the Treasury, during the 180-day period beginning on the date of such submission, disapproves of such table and provides the reasons that such table fails to meet the requirements of clause (iii). The 180-day period shall be extended upon mutual agreement of the Secretary of the Treasury and the plan sponsor.

children.heading: Separate mortality tables for the disabled
children.label: D Separate mortality tables for the disabled

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury shall establish mortality tables which may be used (in lieu of the tables under subparagraph (A)) under this subsection for individuals who are entitled to benefits under the plan on account of disability. The Secretary of the Treasury shall establish separate tables for individuals whose disabilities occur in plan years beginning before , and for individuals whose disabilities occur in plan years beginning on or after such date.January 1, 1995

children.heading: Special rule for disabilities occurring after 1994
children.label: ii Special rule for disabilities occurring after 1994
children.text: In the case of disabilities occurring in plan years beginning after , the tables under clause (i) shall apply only with respect to individuals described in such subclause who are disabled within the meaning of title II of the Social Security Act [ et seq.] and the regulations thereunder.December 31, 199442 U.S.C. 401

children.heading: Periodic revision
children.label: iii Periodic revision
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury shall (at least every 10 years) make revisions in any table in effect under clause (i) to reflect the actual experience of pension plans and projected trends in such experience.

children.label: 5 Approval of large changes in actuarial assumptions

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: No actuarial assumption used to determine the funding target for a plan to which this paragraph applies may be changed without the approval of the Secretary of the Treasury.

children.label: i Special rules for at-risk plans

children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, plan years beginning before 2008 shall not be taken into account.

children.heading: Small plan exception
children.label: 6 Small plan exception
children.text: If, on each day during the preceding plan year, a plan had 500 or fewer participants, the plan shall not be treated as in at-risk status for the plan year. For purposes of this paragraph, all defined benefit plans (other than multiemployer plans) maintained by the same employer (or any member of such employer’s controlled group) shall be treated as 1 plan, but only participants with respect to such employer or member shall be taken into account and the rules of subsection (g)(2)(C) shall apply.

children.heading: Payment of minimum required contributions
children.label: j Payment of minimum required contributions

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For purposes of this section, the due date for any payment of any minimum required contribution for any plan year shall be 8½ months after the close of the plan year.

children.heading: Interest
children.label: 2 Interest
children.text: Any payment required under paragraph (1) for a plan year that is made on a date other than the valuation date for such plan year shall be adjusted for interest accruing for the period between the valuation date and the payment date, at the effective rate of interest for the plan for such plan year.

children.heading: Accelerated quarterly contribution schedule for underfunded plans
children.label: 3 Accelerated quarterly contribution schedule for underfunded plans

children.heading: Failure to timely make required installment
children.label: A Failure to timely make required installment
children.text: In any case in which the plan has a funding shortfall for the preceding plan year, the employer maintaining the plan shall make the required installments under this paragraph and if the employer fails to pay the full amount of a required installment for the plan year, then the amount of interest charged under paragraph (2) on the underpayment for the period of underpayment shall be determined by using a rate of interest equal to the rate otherwise used under paragraph (2) plus 5 percentage points. In the case of plan years beginning in 2008, the funding shortfall for the preceding plan year may be determined using such methods of estimation as the Secretary of the Treasury may provide.

children.heading: Period of underpayment
children.label: ii Period of underpayment
children.text: The period for which any interest is charged under this paragraph with respect to any portion of the underpayment shall run from the due date for the installment to the date on which such portion is contributed to or under the plan.

children.heading: Order of crediting contributions
children.label: iii Order of crediting contributions
children.text: For purposes of clause (i)(II), contributions shall be credited against unpaid required installments in the order in which such installments are required to be paid.

children.heading: Number of required installments; due dates
children.label: C Number of required installments; due dates

children.heading: Payable in 4 installments
children.label: i Payable in 4 installments
children.text: There shall be 4 required installments for each plan year.

children.text: The due dates for required installments are set forth in the following table:In the case of the following required installment:The due date is:1stApril 152ndJuly 153rdOctober 154thJanuary 15 of the following year.

children.text: The amount of any required installment shall be 25 percent of the required annual payment.

children.heading: Fiscal years, short years, and years with alternate valuation date
children.label: E Fiscal years, short years, and years with alternate valuation date

children.heading: Fiscal years
children.label: i Fiscal years
children.text: In applying this paragraph to a plan year beginning on any date other than January 1, there shall be substituted for the months specified in this paragraph, the months which correspond thereto.

children.heading: Short plan year
children.label: ii Short plan year
children.text: This subparagraph shall be applied to plan years of less than 12 months in accordance with regulations prescribed by the Secretary of the Treasury.

children.heading: Plan with alternate valuation date
children.label: iii Plan with alternate valuation date
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury shall prescribe regulations for the application of this paragraph in the case of a plan which has a valuation date other than the first day of the plan year.

children.heading: Quarterly contributions not to include certain increased contributions
children.label: F Quarterly contributions not to include certain increased contributions
children.text: Subparagraph (D) shall be applied without regard to any increase under subsection (c)(7).

children.label: 4 Liquidity requirement in connection with quarterly contributions

children.label: A In general
children.text: A plan to which this paragraph applies shall be treated as failing to pay the full amount of any required installment under paragraph (3) to the extent that the value of the liquid assets paid in such installment is less than the liquidity shortfall (whether or not such liquidity shortfall exceeds the amount of such installment required to be paid but for this paragraph).

i

children.heading: Period of underpayment
children.label: C Period of underpayment
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (3)(A), any portion of an installment that is treated as not paid under subparagraph (A) shall continue to be treated as unpaid until the close of the quarter in which the due date for such installment occurs.

children.heading: Limitation on increase
children.label: D Limitation on increase
children.text: If the amount of any required installment is increased by reason of subparagraph (A), in no event shall such increase exceed the amount which, when added to prior installments for the plan year, is necessary to increase the funding target attainment percentage of the plan for the plan year (taking into account the expected increase in funding target due to benefits accruing or earned during the plan year) to 100 percent.

children.label: I In general
children.text: The term “base amount” means, with respect to any quarter, an amount equal to 3 times the sum of the adjusted disbursements from the plan for the 12 months ending on the last day of such quarter.

children.heading: Special rule
children.label: II Special rule
children.text: If the amount determined under subclause (I) exceeds an amount equal to 2 times the sum of the adjusted disbursements from the plan for the 36 months ending on the last day of the quarter and an enrolled actuary certifies to the satisfaction of the Secretary of the Treasury that such excess is the result of nonrecurring circumstances, the base amount with respect to such quarter shall be determined without regard to amounts related to those nonrecurring circumstances.

children.heading: Disbursements from the plan
children.label: iii Disbursements from the plan
children.text: The term “disbursements from the plan” means all disbursements from the trust, including purchases of annuities, payments of single sums and other benefits, and administrative expenses.

children.text: the sum of the purchases of annuities, payments of single sums, and such other disbursements as the Secretary of the Treasury shall provide in regulations.

children.heading: Liquid assets
children.label: v Liquid assets
children.text: The term “liquid assets” means cash, marketable securities, and such other assets as specified by the Secretary of the Treasury in regulations.

children.heading: Quarter
children.label: vi Quarter
children.text: The term “quarter” means, with respect to any required installment, the 3-month period preceding the month in which the due date for such installment occurs.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: F Regulations
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury may prescribe such regulations as are necessary to carry out this paragraph.

children.heading: Imposition of lien where failure to make required contributions
children.label: k Imposition of lien where failure to make required contributions
A

children.heading: Plans to which subsection applies
children.label: 2 Plans to which subsection applies
children.text: This subsection shall apply to a single-employer plan covered under for any plan year for which the funding target attainment percentage (as defined in subsection (d)(2)) of such plan is less than 100 percent.section 1321 of this title

children.heading: Amount of lien
children.label: 3 Amount of lien
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1), the amount of the lien shall be equal to the aggregate unpaid balance of contribution payments required under this section and for which payment has not been made before the due date.section 1082 of this title

children.heading: Notice of failure
children.label: A Notice of failure
children.text: A person committing a failure described in paragraph (1) shall notify the Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation of such failure within 10 days of the due date for the required contribution payment.

children.heading: Period of lien
children.label: B Period of lien
children.text: The lien imposed by paragraph (1) shall arise on the due date for the required contribution payment and shall continue until the last day of the first plan year in which the plan ceases to be described in paragraph (1)(B). Such lien shall continue to run without regard to whether such plan continues to be described in paragraph (2) during the period referred to in the preceding sentence.

children.heading: Certain rules to apply
children.label: C Certain rules to apply
children.text: Any amount with respect to which a lien is imposed under paragraph (1) shall be treated as taxes due and owing the United States and rules similar to the rules of subsections (c), (d), and (e) of shall apply with respect to a lien imposed by subsection (a) and the amount with respect to such lien.section 1368 of this title5So in original. Probably should be “paragraph (1)”.5

children.heading: Enforcement
children.label: 5 Enforcement
children.text: Any lien created under paragraph (1) may be perfected and enforced only by the Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation, or at the direction of the Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation, by the contributing sponsor (or any member of the controlled group of the contributing sponsor).

children.heading: Contribution payment
children.label: A Contribution payment
children.text: The term “contribution payment” means, in connection with a plan, a contribution payment required to be made to the plan, including any required installment under paragraphs (3) and (4) of subsection (j).

children.heading: Due date; required installment
children.label: B Due date; required installment
children.text: The terms “due date” and “required installment” have the meanings given such terms by subsection (j).

children.heading: Controlled group
children.label: C Controlled group
children.text: The term “controlled group” means any group treated as a single employer under subsections (b), (c), (m), and () of .osection 414 of title 26

children.heading: Qualified transfers to health benefit accounts
children.label: l Qualified transfers to health benefit accounts
children.text: In the case of a qualified transfer (as defined in ), any assets so transferred shall not, for purposes of this section, be treated as assets in the plan.section 420 of title 26

children.heading: Special rules for community newspaper plans
children.label: m Special rules for community newspaper plans

children.label: 1 In general
children.text: An eligible newspaper plan sponsor of a plan under which no participant has had the participant’s accrued benefit increased (whether because of service or compensation) after , may elect to have the alternative standards described in paragraph (4) apply to such plan.April 2, 2019

children.heading: Election
children.label: 3 Election
children.text: An election under paragraph (1) shall be made at such time and in such manner as prescribed by the Secretary of the Treasury. Such election, once made with respect to a plan year, shall apply to all subsequent plan years unless revoked with the consent of the Secretary of the Treasury.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Notwithstanding subsection (h)(2)(C) and except as provided in clause (ii), the first, second, and third segment rates in effect for any month for purposes of this section shall be 8 percent.

children.heading: New benefit accruals
children.label: ii New benefit accruals
children.text: Notwithstanding subsection (h)(2), for purposes of determining the funding target and normal cost of a plan for any plan year, the present value of any benefits accrued or earned under the plan for a plan year with respect to which an election under paragraph (1) is in effect shall be determined on the basis of the United States Treasury obligation yield curve for the day that is the valuation date of such plan for such plan year.

children.heading: United States Treasury obligation yield curve
children.label: iii United States Treasury obligation yield curve
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “United States Treasury obligation yield curve” means, with respect to any day, a yield curve which shall be prescribed by the Secretary of the Treasury for such day on interest-bearing obligations of the United States.

children.heading: Previous shortfall amortization bases
children.label: i Previous shortfall amortization bases
children.text: The shortfall amortization bases determined under subsection (c)(3) for all plan years preceding the first plan year to which the election under paragraph (1) applies (and all shortfall amortization installments determined with respect to such bases) shall be reduced to zero under rules similar to the rules of subsection (c)(6).

children.heading: New shortfall amortization base
children.label: ii New shortfall amortization base
children.text: Notwithstanding subsection (c)(3), the shortfall amortization base for the first plan year to which the election under paragraph (1) applies shall be the funding shortfall of such plan for such plan year (determined using the interest rates as modified under subparagraph (A)).

children.heading: Determination of shortfall amortization installments
children.label: C Determination of shortfall amortization installments

children.heading: 30-year period
children.label: i 30-year period
children.text: Subparagraphs (A) and (B) of subsection (c)(2) shall be applied by substituting “30-plan-year” for “7-plan-year” each place it appears.

children.heading: No special election
children.label: ii No special election
children.text: The election under subparagraph (D) of subsection (c)(2) shall not apply to any plan year to which the election under paragraph (1) applies.

children.heading: Exemption from at-risk treatment
children.label: D Exemption from at-risk treatment
children.text: Subsection (i) shall not apply.

children.heading: Control
children.label: C Control
children.text: A person shall be treated as controlled by another person if such other person possesses, directly or indirectly, the power to direct or cause the direction and management of such person (including the power to elect a majority of the members of the board of directors of such person) through the ownership of voting securities.

children.heading: Controlled group
children.label: 6 Controlled group
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “controlled group” means all persons treated as a single employer under subsection (b), (c), (m), or () of as of .osection 414 of title 26December 20, 2019

children.heading: Effect on premium rate calculation
children.label: 7 Effect on premium rate calculation
children.text: In the case of a plan for which an election is made to apply the alternative standards described in paragraph (3), the additional premium under shall be determined as if such election had not been made.section 1306(a)(3)(E) of this title
